model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Arkansas gets a narrow edge because the efficiency profiles are essentially even but slightly favor the Razorbacks on KenPom (No. 17 vs. No. 22) and Torvik (No. 20 vs. No. 21), with both teams sporting top-10 caliber offenses and similar defensive levels. In a game that likely comes down to a few possessions, Arkansas’ marginally higher adjusted efficiency margin and scoring ceiling make them the slightly safer advance. Wisconsin absolutely has a credible path if it controls tempo and turns this into a half-court execution game, so confidence stays modest.

reasoningStep.title: Round of 32: Wisconsin vs Arkansas
reasoningStep.summary: Lean Arkansas in a near coin-flip based on a small but consistent ratings edge and comparable health outlook, while acknowledging Wisconsin’s strong tempo-control upset path.
evidence: KenPom: Arkansas #17 (AdjEM 25.43; AdjO rank 5; AdjD 102.1) vs Wisconsin #22 (AdjEM 23.4; AdjO rank 11; AdjD 102.0).
Torvik: Arkansas #20 (AdjO rank 5; AdjD rank 50) vs Wisconsin #21 (AdjO rank 8; AdjD rank 48).
Wisconsin availability: BadgersWire (Mar 10, 2026) reported Nolan Winter 'will definitely be available for the NCAA Tournament,' reducing a key Wisconsin injury concern.
Underdog path: Wisconsin can win by controlling tempo and executing in the half court in a low-possession game, making this a lower-confidence pick.

result.title: Wisconsin Badgers' starting forward exits 78-45 win with lower body injury
result.snippet: Wisconsin beat Iowa 84-71. The Wisconsin Badgers cruised to a dominant win over the visiting Maryland Terrapins on Senior Night, but not without a new concern arising. During the big 78-45 win over the Terrapins, starting Wisconsin forward Nolan Winter exited the game after 21 minutes with what appeared to be an ankle injury .

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://badgerswire.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/badgers/mens-basketball/2026/03/10/wisconsin-greg-gard-nolan-winter-injury/89083341007/ and extracted 1870 characters of text.

result.text: Wisconsin basketball Greg Gard updates Nolan Winter's injury statuys Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Greg Gard provides updates on pair of injured Wisconsin Badgers Jordon Lawrenz Badgers Wire The Wisconsin Badgers head into the Big Ten Tournament as the No. 5 seed, but they're also keeping a close eye on the tournament to follow. Winning the conference tournament is certainly within reach, especially with the way this team has been playing. That said, coach Greg Gard is also focused on the NCAA Tournament , where the Badgers should have some reinforcements. When speaking to the media ahead of the team's quest to win four games in four days at the United Center, Gard told reporters Nolan Winter will definitely be available for the NCAA Tournament. That is music to Badgers fans' ears. The 7-foot junior is a massive part of this Wisconsin lineup. He has started all 30 of the games he's played in this season. Last season, he started 37 games as a sophomore. Sophomore guard Jack Janicki could return as well. Gard's comments were nowhere near as confident as they were for Winter, but the Badgers coach said it's a possibility he could be good to go. Janicki has played in 26 games this season but underwent wrist surgery, which has kept him out of action since Feb. 17. Getting one of those two back for the tournament would be amazing, but having both back in time could change everything. Contact/Follow @TheBadgersWire on X (formerly Twitter) and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of Wisconsin Badgers news, notes and opinion . modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
